name: ALPR configuration: env vars, runtime settings, status endpoint
summary: Master configuration plumbing for the ALPR pipeline. ALPR is opt-in via a runtime settings flag (alpr_enabled, default false) -- the user toggles it from the UI without touching .env. The flag is the single source of truth that workers, API handlers, and the frontend all consult. Env vars only configure deployment-time concerns (engine URL, encryption key, default tunables). Encryption key is the one hard prerequisite: the PUT endpoint refuses to set enabled=true while ALPR_ENCRYPTION_KEY is unset, because there is no safe way to store plate text without it. This feature must land BEFORE workers and API features so they can all reference the same flag and config helpers.
acceptance_criteria: internal/config gains an ALPRConfig struct: EngineURL string, Region string (us|eu|uk|other), FramesPerSecond float64, ConfidenceMin float64, EncryptionKeyB64 string, RetentionDaysUnflagged int, RetentionDaysFlagged int, ExtractorConcurrency int, DetectorConcurrency int, NotifyMinSeverity int, NotifyEmail/Webhook fields. Loaded from env at startup with defaults: ALPR_ENGINE_URL='http://alpr:8081', ALPR_REGION='us', ALPR_FPS=2, ALPR_CONFIDENCE_MIN=0.75, ALPR_RETENTION_DAYS_UNFLAGGED=30, ALPR_RETENTION_DAYS_FLAGGED=365, ALPR_EXTRACTOR_CONCURRENCY=1, ALPR_DETECTOR_CONCURRENCY=2, ALPR_NOTIFY_MIN_SEVERITY=4. EncryptionKeyB64 has no default -- empty means feature cannot be enabled.

internal/settings gains alpr_enabled (bool, default false) and runtime overrides for: region, frames_per_second, confidence_min, retention_days_unflagged, retention_days_flagged, notify_min_severity. Read precedence: settings table > env > default.

New endpoint GET /v1/settings/alpr (session-or-JWT for read; the device may want to know if ALPR is enabled): returns {enabled, engine_url, region, frames_per_second, confidence_min, retention_days_unflagged, retention_days_flagged, encryption_key_configured: bool, engine_reachable: bool, disclaimer_required: bool, disclaimer_version, disclaimer_acked_at}. Never returns the encryption key itself or any plate-related secrets. engine_reachable is the result of a recent (cached <30s) probe of <engine_url>/health.

New endpoint PUT /v1/settings/alpr (session-only): body accepts a subset of the GET shape minus computed fields. Validates: enabled=true requires encryption_key_configured AND disclaimer_acked_at not null AND disclaimer_version current -- otherwise 412 Precondition Failed with {error, missing_prerequisites: ["encryption_key"|"disclaimer"]}. frames_per_second must be in [0.5, 4]. confidence_min must be in [0.5, 0.95]. retention values >= 0.

Routes always register when the schema migration has run, regardless of alpr_enabled value. Workers and handlers gate behavior on the runtime flag (other features), not on whether routes exist. This makes the on/off transition truly seamless from the UI.

Startup: log one info line summarizing the ALPR config when the runtime flag is true; log nothing when false (opt-in is the expected default and should not produce noise). If ALPR_ENCRYPTION_KEY is set but malformed (not valid base64 or <32 bytes), log a warning at startup so the user catches the misconfiguration before trying to enable.
Backend tests: PUT validates each precondition (412 cases), boundary cases for fps/confidence (400), happy-path enable, GET shape stability.

Follow the existing settings pattern in internal/api/settings/* and the GET/PUT /v1/settings/retention handlers. Match the registration site (cmd/server/routes.go) and middleware groupings (sessionOrJWT for read, session for write). Use checkDongleAccess where applicable; this endpoint is server-wide so no dongle check is needed.
notes: Implemented in cmd/server/alpr.go (seed + startup log), internal/config/alpr.go (ALPRConfig + env loader + AES-256 key validation), internal/settings/alpr.go (key constants + bool/float/time helpers), internal/api/settings_alpr.go (GET/PUT /v1/settings/alpr handler with engine_reachable probe and prerequisite gating). Routes wired in cmd/server/routes.go. The disclaimer_acked_at precondition uses settings.KeyALPRDisclaimerAckedAt + settings.KeyALPRDisclaimerVersion -- writing those rows is owned by the still-pending alpr-disclaimer-gate feature, so enable=true currently fails with missing_prerequisites: ["disclaimer"] until that feature lands (intentional ordering).
